# Agent Instructions

## Auto-generated files — do NOT commit

| File / Directory | Generated by |
|---|---|
| `README.md` | `scripts/build.py`, committed back to `main` by the CI bot |
| `dist/` | `scripts/build.py`, deployed to GitHub Pages by CI |

**Never run `scripts/build.py` and commit its output.** The GitHub Actions workflow (`build-and-deploy.yml`) runs the build on every push to `main` and commits `README.md` automatically.

## Source files — edit these

- `data/*.yaml` — tool listings (one file per category)
- `templates/README.md.j2` — Jinja2 template for the README
- `scripts/build.py` — build script
- `scripts/requirements.txt` — Python dependencies
- `site/` — frontend (HTML, CSS, JS)
- `CONTRIBUTING.md` — contributor guidelines

## Tool schema (`data/*.yaml`)

```yaml
- name: Tool Name
  url: https://example.com
  description: One-sentence description.
  location: Country or "Open Source"
  free_tier: What the free tier includes.
```

## Workflow for adding new tools

1. Verify the URL is correct and accessible.
2. Visit the URL and do web research to confirm the description, location (country), and free tier details are accurate.
3. Edit the relevant `data/*.yaml` file to add the tool.

## Workflow

1. Edit the relevant `data/*.yaml` (or template/scripts/site) files.
2. Open a PR — CI will regenerate `README.md` and redeploy the site on merge to `main`.
